criticalBugFix.finding: The critical auth re-login bug is FIXED. Users can now register with a passkey, logout, and successfully re-login with the same passkey without requiring re-registration.
criticalBugFix.evidence: Full browser automation test passed - Register → Login → Logout → Re-login flow works correctly

emailMigration.finding: Email-based authentication successfully implemented. Database schema migrated from username to email column with UNIQUE constraint. All 1,087 existing users have email addresses.
emailMigration.evidence: Schema shows email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, no username column remains

duplicatePrevention.finding: Duplicate email registration prevention works correctly. Incomplete registrations can be resumed, completed registrations are protected from duplicates.
duplicatePrevention.evidence: Multiple register/begin calls for same email return same user ID without creating duplicates

sessionHandling.finding: Session expiry forces re-authentication correctly. Concurrent session handling supports multiple devices per user (867 sessions for 1,079 users).
sessionHandling.evidence: Invalid/expired cookies return {authenticated: false}, database supports multiple refresh_sessions per user

integration.finding: Proxy correctly forwards auth cookies to upstream. Database persistence verified with 1.1MB SQLite file containing all auth data.
integration.evidence: Proxy health check OK, verbose curl shows cookies forwarded, database has users/credentials/sessions tables

frictionsEncountered.description: Auth database file was initially 0 bytes (empty), causing credential storage to fail
frictionsEncountered.resolution: Restarted auth service to properly initialize database schema

frictionsEncountered.description: Database path mismatch - tests were querying wrong database location
Auth service was running old binary with 'username' field instead of 'email'
frictionsEncountered.resolution: Restarted auth service with current code (go run ./cmd/auth)

appliedUpdates.description: Marked 11 assertions as passed for auth-repair milestone

notes: All critical auth-repair assertions passed. The re-login bug fix is verified working through full browser automation. Email migration complete with proper validation and duplicate prevention.
